page: Amazon Web Services c6a.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server powered by AMD EPYC 7R13 processors. It features 192 vCPUs across 96 physical cores using the x86_64 architecture, running at a clock speed of 3.6 GHz. The server is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, relying instead on network-attached storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

In benchmark testing, the c6a.metal demonstrates top-tier performance, particularly in multi-core CPU execution and memory bandwidth. The stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU benchmarks both rank in the top 10%, supported by a top-tier PassMark CPU score and strong multi-core Geekbench results. Memory performance is highly efficient, with small-block read-only memory bandwidth reaching the top 10% tier, while large-block read and write operations remain strong in the top 25%. It also excels in specialized tasks, achieving top-tier results in software compilation with Clang, ray tracing, and cryptographic encryption tests.

While the server delivers high-throughput capabilities for parallel processing, its single-threaded performance in tasks like gzip compression and bzip3 decompression falls into the average tier. Additionally, the lack of bundled local storage and GPU hardware limits its direct applicability for local-storage-heavy databases or GPU-accelerated deep learning. However, the high core density and strong memory bandwidth make it highly cost-efficient for compute-bound, non-GPU workloads. It is highly suited for high-performance computing, large-scale software compilation, ray tracing, Redis-based in-memory caching, and static web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services c6a.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server featuring 192 vCPUs across 96 physical AMD EPYC 7R13 cores on the x86_64 architecture. Operating at 3.6 GHz, the system is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core, and features a 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. It contains no local storage or GPUs. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core CPU performance, PassMark scores, and small-block memory bandwidth, alongside strong software compilation and ray tracing capabilities. Single-threaded compression tasks perform in the average tier. Given its high core density and dedicated CPU allocation, this server is highly cost-efficient for compute-heavy workloads. It is best suited for high-performance computing, large-scale software compilation, Redis caching, and static web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6a.metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server with 192 vCPUs, 384 GB DDR4 RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core CPU performance for demanding enterprise workloads.

tagline: A high-density, bare-metal compute platform featuring ninety-six physical AMD EPYC cores and fifty gigabits per second baseline network bandwidth.

Equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
